task_completion.description: Model the barge with a beam of 10m and height of 6m, centered horizontally with the keel at y=0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rectangle or polygon visible in the GeoGebra graphics or algebra view, spanning from x=-5 to x=5 and y=0 to y=6.

task_completion.description: Plot the center of gravity (KG) at y=4.5m and the draft at y=3m.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A point plotted at (0, 4.5) representing the center of gravity, and a horizontal line or segment at y=3 representing the draft/waterline.

task_completion.description: Simulate a 15-degree counter-clockwise heel by rotating the hull around the center of flotation (0, 3).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second polygon or set of lines representing the hull rotated 15 degrees counter-clockwise. The rotation should clearly be centered at the point (0, 3).

task_completion.description: Determine the new center of buoyancy (B') and the metacenter (M), and measure the metacentric height (GM).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Points in the graphics or algebra view representing the new center of buoyancy (e.g., labeled B') and the metacenter (e.g., labeled M). There should also be a measurement, segment, or calculation representing the metacentric height (GM).

task_completion.description: Add a text annotation stating whether the ship is 'Stable' or 'Unstable'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box in the GeoGebra graphics view containing the word 'Unstable'.
The GeoGebra window title shows 'barge_stability.ggb' or a save dialog is visible with this exact filename.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GeoGebra GUI to construct the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GeoGebra application open with tools being used, rather than a terminal or text editor creating a .ggb file via scripts.

integrity.description: The geometric objects are genuinely constructed in GeoGebra.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view shows the mathematical definitions of the polygons, points, and rotations, rather than just arbitrary unlinked drawings.

integrity.description: The text annotation is placed within the GeoGebra application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The word 'Unstable' is a text object within the GeoGebra graphics view, not typed into an external text editor or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Barge dimensions: Beam (width) = 10.0, Height = 6.0. Heel angle: 15.0 degrees. Expected filename: 'barge_stability.ggb'. Based on the physics of the problem, the ship is 'Unstable', so the text annotation should state 'Unstable'.
